seo.title: AI Agent Cost Optimization in BeeAI Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent cost optimization in BeeAI agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: AI Agent Cost Optimization issues in BeeAI manifest through unbounded memory growth during long sessions, inefficient tool calls without caching, excessive Granite model token usage from format inconsistencies, and redundant state serializations. These problems lead to skyrocketing inference costs and unpredictable billing in production deployments. BeeAI's event-driven architecture amplifies these issues as agents scale across multiple concurrent sessions.

howItManifests.symptom: Agent memory balloons from 100MB to 5GB+ after 2 hours
howItManifests.impact: Token costs increase 50x due to massive context windows

howItManifests.scenario: Tool-heavy research agents with repeated API calls
howItManifests.symptom: Same tool called 100+ times without caching
Model outputs rejected 40% of time requiring retries
howItManifests.impact: 3x inference cost multiplier from repeated generations

howItManifests.symptom: Full conversation history serialized every turn
howItManifests.impact: Database write costs grow linearly with conversation length

detection.name: Memory Growth Monitor
detection.description: Detects unbounded memory growth by tracking agent memory usage over time

detection.name: Token Waste Analyzer
detection.description: Identifies excessive token usage from retries and format issues

detection.name: Tool Call Duplication Detector
detection.description: Finds repeated tool calls that should be cached
detection.code:
from beeai.events import ToolCallEvent
from collections import defaultdict

class ToolDuplicationDetector:
    def __init__(self, window_minutes=30):
        self.tool_calls = defaultdict(list)
        self.window = window_minutes * 60

    def on_tool_call(self, event: ToolCallEvent):
        now = event.timestamp
        tool_signature = (event.tool_name, str(event.args))

        # Clean old calls
        self.tool_calls[tool_signature] = [
            call for call in self.tool_calls[tool_signature]
            if now - call.timestamp < self.window
        ]

        self.tool_calls[tool_signature].append(event)

        if len(self.tool_calls[tool_signature]) > 5:
            print(f'🚨 TOOL DUPLICATION: {event.tool_name} called {len(self.tool_calls[tool_signature])} times in {self.window/60}m')

fixes.name: Memory Truncation Middleware
fixes.description: Automatically truncates conversation history to control memory growth
fixes.code:
from beeai import Agent, Middleware
from beeai.events import MessageEvent

class MemoryOptimizerMiddleware(Middleware):
    def __init__(self, max_memory_mb=500, max_messages=50):
        self.max_memory_mb = max_memory_mb
        self.max_messages = max_messages

    def before_message(self, agent: Agent, event: MessageEvent):
        memory_mb = agent.get_memory_usage()
        messages = agent.conversation_history

        if memory_mb > self.max_memory_mb or len(messages) > self.max_messages:
            # Keep only recent messages
            recent_messages = messages[-self.max_messages//2:]
            agent.conversation_history = recent_messages
            agent.clear_memory_cache()
            print(f'✅ Memory optimized: {len(messages)} → {len(recent_messages)} messages')

fixes.name: Tool Call Cache
fixes.description: Caches identical tool calls to eliminate duplication

fixes.name: Granite Format Normalizer
fixes.description: Standardizes Granite model outputs to prevent parsing retries

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Cost Optimization Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly with BeeAI's event system, automatically detecting cost optimization opportunities across all your agents. By registering as an event listener, Vex monitors memory growth, token waste, and tool duplication in real-time, providing actionable insights and automated fixes. 

Vex's cost optimization agent runs continuous analysis, identifying the top 3 cost drivers in your BeeAI deployment and applying fixes with zero-downtime. It learns from your usage patterns to set intelligent thresholds and prioritizes fixes by dollar impact. 

The integration requires just 5 lines of code and works across all BeeAI agents, providing a centralized dashboard of your cost savings and optimization recommendations.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk import Vex
from beeai import Agent
from beeai.events import EventHandler

# Install: pip install beeai-framework vex-sdk

vex = Vex(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

@vex.register_optimization_suite('beeai-cost-optimizer')
def cost_optimizer_suite():
    return {
        'memory_growth': {'threshold': '2x', 'auto_fix': True},
        'token_waste': {'max_retries': 2, 'auto_fix': True},
        'tool_duplication': {'window': '30m', 'auto_fix': True}
    }

# Auto-register with all BeeAI agents
EventHandler.register_global_listener(vex.event_listener)

# Vex auto-deploys optimization rules
vex.deploy_suite('beeai-cost-optimizer')
print('🚀 Vex Cost Optimizer active - monitoring all BeeAI agents')
